title: Auto-retry GPU connect — scarce-card retry hardening (regex + spacing + connecting label + wait-ownership + OPEN: button flicker)
description:
BUG (MPI-110 follow-up): With 'Auto-retry connection' ON, pressing Connect for a scarce GPU (RTX 5090, Any-region) showed the 'Could not connect to a Pod.' toast and did NOT retry. Button stayed Connect (never became Cancel), no background wait armed.

TWO DEFECTS (both fixed this session):

=== Defect 1: classifier missed RunPod's wording ===
RunPod refuses a scarce-card create with 'This machine does not have the resources to deploy your pod. Please try a different machine' (HTTP 500 -> frontend 502). The out-of-stock classifier regex /not enough|unavailable|no .*available|out of stock|insufficient/i did NOT match that wording, so `outOfStock`/`refusedOutOfStock` was false -> retry handoff (`_handoffToWait`) skipped -> dead-end toast. Confirmed logs/app.log L2014-2046 (7 consecutive `__any__` 5090 refusals, exact string). Same narrow regex in BOTH the Settings connect path (MpiSettings.js ~L755) AND the boot auto-connect loop (shell.js ~L628) -> neither retried.
FIX 1: shared `_isStockRefusal(msg)` helper in BOTH shell.js (module fn near _BOOT_RETRY_INTERVAL_MS) and MpiSettings.js (module fn near REUSE_PARTS), broadened to also match: 'does not have the resources', 'no longer any instances', 'try a different machine', 'no instances available'. Replaced the two inline regex copies. Real-failure strings (Invalid API key / offline / Volume not found / Internal server error) deliberately do NOT match so they still surface.

=== Defect 2: wait loop HOT-SPUN (no backoff) ===
After FIX 1, retry engaged (log line 'sniped mid-create -- re-waiting (auto-retry)' appeared) BUT it spun ~every 1-2s instead of every 15s. CAUSE: _startGpuWait gated onFree() on `_isGpuInStockBoot` (RunPod's `available` flag). That flag is OPTIMISTIC -- a scarce 5090 reads available:true even when createPod refuses. So the gate returned true on tick 1 -> fired the create immediately -> refused -> caller re-entered the wait -> flag STILL true -> fired again with NO sleep. UI flickered waiting<->creating; API hammered. Seen live logs/app.log L2085-2091 (attempts seconds apart).
FIX 2 (shell.js _startGpuWait, ~L471): inverted the loop. Always sleep _BOOT_RETRY_INTERVAL_MS (15s) BEFORE each attempt; the create attempt itself is the only ground-truth probe (the `available` flag lies for scarce cards). A refusal flows back as another SPACED wait iteration. Self-check (waitloop model): onFree never fires before a backoff; 5 refuse-retries take 5*15s not a spin; abort/intent-change exits without firing onFree -- all PASS.

VERIFY: regex self-check 12/12 PASS (incl. exact log string + non-retryable surfacing); wait-loop backoff self-check PASS; tests/runpod-remote-hardening.test.cjs 16/16 pass; eslint clean.
LIVE verify OWED (could not test this session -- user got a card on the FIRST try, no stock pressure to trigger retry): with auto-retry on + a genuinely scarce card, confirm button->Cancel + 'Waiting for <gpu>' stays steady ~15s between attempts (NO flicker) + eventual connect. FIX 1's retry-engagement WAS confirmed live (the re-waiting log line); only FIX 2's spacing is unverified live.

=== Defect 3: panel status said 'creating...' during the CONNECTING phase ===
User flagged: after a Pod is created and is pulling its image/booting, the app IS connecting, but the Settings panel status text stayed 'creating...' the whole time (hero hero phase already showed CONNECTING). Two surfaces disagreeing about the same truth; the panel was wrong. 'creating' should mean 'asking RunPod to make the Pod'; once the Pod EXISTS and we poll it to ready, that's 'connecting'.
FIX 3 (MpiSettings.js, 4 spots, all guarded by NOT-warm so warm-resume keeps 'resuming...'): create->poll-to-ready (~L788) creating->connecting; WS-handshake (~L857) creating->connecting; WS-not-yet (~L867) creating->connecting; panel-restore running-but-not-ready (~L578) creating->connecting. Left as 'creating...' deliberately: the !ready TIMEOUT/retry branch (~L840) because it pairs with a 'Connect' button (nothing actively connecting). Result: the active connecting window now reads creating(pre-Pod, hint/toast only) -> connecting... -> ready, matching the hero. eslint clean.

=== Defect 4: duplicate wait driver (was briefly carded as MPI-138, now merged here) ===
FIX 1+2 live-verified 2026-06-25 (logs/app.log L2243-2275 / L2285+): retry engages on every 'no resources' refusal and attempts are ~15s apart (spin gone). GOOD. But two retry drivers run for the same GPU: the manual Settings Connect AND _initRemoteBoot (logged as 'auto-connect-on-start', fires when autoConnectOnStart is on). The remote:wait-start handler stop+restarted any live wait unconditionally.
FIX 4 (shell.js _initGpuWaitBridge): added module var _gpuWaitFor tracking which GPU the live wait covers; a wait-start for the SAME gpu now no-ops instead of stop+restart. Self-check 5/5 PASS; 16/16 hardening; eslint clean. NOTE: this guard is correct hygiene but did NOT fix the visible flicker (see Defect 5) -- the flicker has a different cause. Kept (harmless, prevents a real duplicate-wait), revisit in the Defect-5 redesign.

=== Defect 5: button flicker waiting -> 'creating' -> stopped/Connect -> waiting, every 15s -- FIXED on disk (Option A), live-verify owed ===
FIX 5 (shell.js, this session, Option A = silent retry): root cause is remoteWaitGpu — the panel's ONLY flicker driver (MpiSettings onState('remoteWaitGpu') -> _applyEngineStatus). It was nulled before each create attempt, and _initRemoteBoot announced 'connecting'/'Creating' BEFORE the create. Three edits: (a) _startGpuWait no longer nulls remoteWaitGpu before onFree; (b) added _announceConnecting() in _initRemoteBoot that clears remoteWaitGpu + emits phase:'connecting' + 'Creating a Pod' toast ONLY once a real podId lands (warm resume announces immediately; fresh create defers to after a successful create response); (c) a refused create now backs off INLINE (15s, remoteWaitGpu stays pinned to the gpu and doubles as the live abort signal — a user Cancel via _stopGpuWait or a GPU-switch clears it and we bail) instead of bouncing to a nested _startGpuWait. That nested wait was the real defect: when the Settings handoff drove _initRemoteBoot the OUTER _startGpuWait was still active, so the nested one bailed immediately (one-wait guard) -> freed=false -> retry died after ONE refusal, and each 15s tick re-invoked a fresh LOUD _initRemoteBoot = the flicker. Net: remoteWaitGpu stays set through ALL refused attempts -> panel holds steady on 'waiting…'; a single clean waiting->connecting transition fires only when a Pod exists. Self-check waitloop.mjs (flicker invariant: transitions == [gpu,null], announce-once, all-refuse never clears) 5/5 PASS; tests/runpod-remote-hardening.test.cjs 16/16; eslint clean. NOT committed yet. LIVE verify OWED: scarce 5090 + auto-retry on -> button stays Cancel/'waiting…' steady across multiple 15s ticks (NO cycle to Creating/stopped), then a clean single flip to connecting when it frees.

--- original OPEN trace (historical, pre-fix) ---
Live 2026-06-25 (post-restart, build with fixes 1-4): with auto-retry on + scarce 5090, the Connect button does NOT stay steady on Cancel/'waiting...'. Every ~15s it cycles: 'waiting...' -> 'Creating a Pod...' (Cancel) -> 'stopped' (Connect reappears) -> back to 'waiting...'. User pressed Cancel zero times.
FULL TRACE (root cause): the Settings retry hands off via the shell wait's onFree = _initRemoteBoot (shell.js _runSettingsWait ~L524). _initRemoteBoot is a LOUD top-level connect: BEFORE its create loop it emits StatusBar.notify('Creating a Pod...') + _emitRemoteConnection({phase:'connecting'}) (~L637-638); on a refusal inside the loop it emits _emitRemoteConnection({phase:null}) (~L655) then re-waits. So EACH 15s retry cycle invokes a fresh loud _initRemoteBoot -> repaints the whole connect lifecycle: connecting (creating/Cancel) -> phase:null (stopped/Connect flash) -> re-wait (waiting). Contrast the boot path's INTERNAL re-wait which uses onFree: async()=>{} (SILENT no-op, shell.js ~L658) and does NOT re-announce. The asymmetry (Settings handoff = loud _initRemoteBoot per tick; boot internal = silent) is the flicker source. FIX 1's spacing and FIX 4's dup-guard do not touch this.
REDESIGN DIRECTION (do deliberately next session, with the MPI-73 phase-emit model in hand -- two patch attempts already shifted the symptom without killing it):
  Option A (preferred): make the per-retry create SILENT -- stay on 'waiting...' through refused attempts; only emit 'connecting' / 'Creating a Pod' when a create actually RETURNS A POD (podId present), not on every attempt. i.e. move the announce from before-the-loop to after-a-successful-create.
  Option B: single owner -- Settings hands to ONE _initRemoteBoot whose internal loop runs silently to completion; remove the outer Settings re-wait so there's no nested _startGpuWait re-announcing.
Either touches MPI-73's phase/status emit model -- treat as a real change, not a hot patch.

Files touched so far: js/shell.js, js/components/Compounds/LandingPages/MpiSettings/MpiSettings.js. Branch: RunPod. Commits: 1d42ad2 (fixes 1-3), 95c8a05 (fix 4).

STATE: in 'doing'/in-progress. Fixes 1-4 on disk + committed; 1-2 live-verified working; 3 (connecting label) + 4 (dup guard) logic-verified, live-unconfirmed. Defect 5 (flicker) OPEN -- functionally retry works (lands the card), flicker is a UX defect to redesign. This is the SINGLE owner card for the whole auto-retry-connect issue (MPI-138 merged in; MPI-135 stays separate = DC-steering + bad-host, a different concern). Move to done only after the flicker redesign + a clean live verify.
